arguments.file_text:
# Migration Plan: VSTest → Microsoft.Testing.Platform

## Problem
5 MSTest projects currently use the legacy VSTest runner infrastructure. Goal is to migrate to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP), update Azure DevOps pipelines, and preserve TRX reporting + code coverage.

## Approach
MSTest 3.x already bundles MTP support — no new test-framework package is needed. The migration is mostly project-file changes, a runner opt-in, and pipeline adjustments.

---

## Phase 1 — Project file changes (per test project)

### 1.1 Update packages
Upgrade to the latest MSTest meta-package and drop the split references:

```xml
<!-- REMOVE these three -->
<PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.8.0" />
<PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.8.0" />
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.13.0" />

<!-- ADD the unified package (4.x ships MTP by default) -->
<PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="4.3.0" />
```

> **Why MSTest 4.x?** Starting with MSTest 4.0.0 the `MSTest` meta-package enables MTP by default, so you don't need the manual `<EnableMSTestRunner>` property. It also drops the implicit dependency on `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` (the VSTest glue).

### 1.2 Set OutputType
Each test `.csproj` must produce an exe (MTP tests are self-contained executables):

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
</PropertyGroup>
```

> If you're on MSTest 4.x this is set automatically and can be omitted.

### 1.3 (Alternative) Stay on MSTest 3.8 and opt in manually
If you prefer a smaller version bump:

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
</PropertyGroup>
```

Keep `MSTest.TestFramework` + `MSTest.TestAdapter` 3.8.0 and keep `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` (it enables `dotnet test` compatibility).

**Recommendation:** Go straight to MSTest 4.x — it's the clean-break path and avoids straddling two runners.

### 1.4 TRX reporting
Add the TRX report extension so `--results-directory` keeps working:

```xml
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.2" />
```

Enable it in each project (or in `Directory.Build.props`):

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <TestingExtensionsTrxReport>true</TestingExtensionsTrxReport>
</PropertyGroup>
```

### 1.5 Code coverage
Replace the old `coverlet` / VSTest collector approach:

```xml
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.2" />
```

Enable:

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <TestingExtensionsCodeCoverage>true</TestingExtensionsCodeCoverage>
</PropertyGroup>
```

This uses the Microsoft code-coverage collector that integrates natively with MTP. It produces `.cobertura.xml` by default.

### 1.6 Centralise in Directory.Build.props (recommended)
Put the shared settings in a single `Directory.Build.props` at the test-project root:

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <TestingExtensionsTrxReport>true</TestingExtensionsTrxReport>
    <TestingExtensionsCodeCoverage>true</TestingExtensionsCodeCoverage>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="4.3.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.2" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.2" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

---

## Phase 2 — Remove legacy artifacts

### 2.1 Delete `.runsettings` (if only used for VSTest adapter config)
MTP doesn't use `.runsettings`. If your runsettings only configured MSTest/VSTest adapter knobs, delete it. If it has code-coverage include/exclude filters, migrate those to a `.codecoverage` JSON config file or MSBuild properties.

### 2.2 Remove any `[assembly: Parallelize]` → replace with MSBuild property
```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <TestingParallelism>auto</TestingParallelism>
</PropertyGroup>
```

---

## Phase 3 — Local verification

### 3.1 Build
```bash
dotnet build
```

### 3.2 Run tests via `dotnet test`
```bash
dotnet test --results-directory ./TestResults
```

Confirm:
- All 5 projects produce a TRX file under `./TestResults`.
- All tests pass.

### 3.3 Run tests via `dotnet run` (native MTP mode)
```bash
dotnet run --project Tests/MyTests.csproj -- --report-trx --results-directory ./TestResults
```

This proves the exe-based runner works directly.

### 3.4 Verify code coverage
```bash
dotnet test --collect "Microsoft.CodeCoverage"
```

Check that `.cobertura.xml` (or `.coverage`) files appear in `TestResults/`.

---

## Phase 4 — Azure DevOps pipeline changes

### 4.1 Replace `VSTest@3` with `DotNetCoreCLI@2`

```yaml
# BEFORE
- task: VSTest@3
  inputs:
    testAssemblyVer2: '**\*Tests.dll'
    runSettingsFile: 'test.runsettings'
    codeCoverageEnabled: true

# AFTER
- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
  displayName: 'Run tests'
  inputs:
    command: 'test'
    projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'
    arguments: >-
      --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults
      --no-build
      --configuration Release
```

### 4.2 Publish TRX results

```yaml
- task: PublishTestResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish test results'
  condition: always()
  inputs:
    testResultsFormat: 'VSTest'   # TRX format
    testResultsFiles: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/**/*.trx'
    mergeTestResults: true
```

### 4.3 Publish code coverage

```yaml
- task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish code coverage'
  condition: always()
  inputs:
    summaryFileLocation: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/**/coverage.cobertura.xml'
```

---

## Phase 5 — Cleanup & hardening

- [ ] Remove any remaining `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` references (MSTest 4.x doesn't need it).
- [ ] Remove `MSTest.TestAdapter` and `MSTest.TestFramework` individual references.
- [ ] Delete `.runsettings` if no longer needed.
- [ ] Pin package versions in `Directory.Packages.props` if using Central Package Management.
- [ ] Update CI caches if they key on package hashes.

---

## Risks / Notes

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Tests that rely on VSTest-specific APIs (`TestContext.DeploymentDirectory`, deployment items) | Audit usage; MTP doesn't support deployment items — copy files via build targets instead. |
| Custom VSTest loggers | Replace with MTP extensions or the TRX extension above. |
| `.runsettings` with complex coverage filters | Migrate to `microsoft.codecoverage.json` config file. |
| Parallel test execution differences | MTP runs each project exe in parallel by default; within-project parallelism needs `<TestingParallelism>`. |
| Third-party analyzers that hook into VSTest | Verify compatibility; most major tools support MTP now. |

---

## TODO Summary

1. Update package references in all 5 test projects (or `Directory.Build.props`)
2. Remove old VSTest packages (`MSTest.TestAdapter`, `MSTest.TestFramework`, `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`)
3. Add TRX + CodeCoverage extension packages
4. Remove/migrate `.runsettings`
5. Verify locally — build, test, TRX output, coverage output
6. Update Azure DevOps pipeline: replace `VSTest@3` → `DotNetCoreCLI@2` + `PublishTestResults@2` + `PublishCodeCoverageResults@2`
7. Clean up obsolete files and references

# Migration Plan: VSTest → Microsoft.Testing.Platform

## Problem
5 MSTest projects currently use the legacy VSTest runner infrastructure. Goal is to migrate to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP), update Azure DevOps pipelines, and preserve TRX reporting + code coverage.

## Approach
MSTest 3.x already bundles MTP support — no new test-framework package is needed. The migration is mostly project-file changes, a runner opt-in, and pipeline adjustments.

---

## Phase 1 — Project file changes (per test project)

### 1.1 Update packages
Upgrade to the latest MSTest meta-package and drop the split references:

```xml
<!-- REMOVE these three -->
<PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.8.0" />
<PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.8.0" />
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.13.0" />

<!-- ADD the unified package (4.x ships MTP by default) -->
<PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="4.3.0" />
```

> **Why MSTest 4.x?** Starting with MSTest 4.0.0 the `MSTest` meta-package enables MTP by default, so you don't need the manual `<EnableMSTestRunner>` property. It also drops the implicit dependency on `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` (the VSTest glue).

### 1.2 Set OutputType
Each test `.csproj` must produce an exe (MTP tests are self-contained executables):

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
</PropertyGroup>
```

> If you're on MSTest 4.x this is set automatically and can be omitted.

### 1.3 (Alternative) Stay on MSTest 3.8 and opt in manually
If you prefer a smaller version bump:

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
</PropertyGroup>
```

Keep `MSTest.TestFramework` + `MSTest.TestAdapter` 3.8.0 and keep `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` (it enables `dotnet test` compatibility).

**Recommendation:** Go straight to MSTest 4.x — it's the clean-break path and avoids straddling two runners.

### 1.4 TRX reporting
Add the TRX report extension so `--results-directory` keeps working:

```xml
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.2" />
```

Enable it in each project (or in `Directory.Build.props`):

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <TestingExtensionsTrxReport>true</TestingExtensionsTrxReport>
</PropertyGroup>
```

### 1.5 Code coverage
Replace the old coverlet / VSTest collector approach:

```xml
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.2" />
```

Enable:

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <TestingExtensionsCodeCoverage>true</TestingExtensionsCodeCoverage>
</PropertyGroup>
```

This uses the Microsoft code-coverage collector that integrates natively with MTP. It produces `.cobertura.xml` by default.

### 1.6 Centralise in Directory.Build.props (recommended)
Put shared settings in a single `Directory.Build.props` at the test-project root:

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <TestingExtensionsTrxReport>true</TestingExtensionsTrxReport>
    <TestingExtensionsCodeCoverage>true</TestingExtensionsCodeCoverage>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="4.3.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.2" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.2" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

---

## Phase 2 — Remove legacy artifacts

### 2.1 Delete `.runsettings` (if only used for VSTest adapter config)
MTP doesn't use `.runsettings`. If your runsettings only configured MSTest/VSTest adapter knobs, delete it. If it has code-coverage include/exclude filters, migrate those to a `.codecoverage` JSON config file or MSBuild properties.

### 2.2 Remove any `[assembly: Parallelize]` → replace with MSBuild property
```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <TestingParallelism>auto</TestingParallelism>
</PropertyGroup>
```

---

## Phase 3 — Local verification

### 3.1 Build
```bash
dotnet build
```

### 3.2 Run tests via `dotnet test`
```bash
dotnet test --results-directory ./TestResults
```

Confirm:
- All 5 projects produce a TRX file under `./TestResults`.
- All tests pass.

### 3.3 Run tests via `dotnet run` (native MTP mode)
```bash
dotnet run --project Tests/MyTests.csproj -- --report-trx --results-directory ./TestResults
```

This proves the exe-based runner works directly.

### 3.4 Verify code coverage
```bash
dotnet test --collect "Microsoft.CodeCoverage"
```

Check that `.cobertura.xml` (or `.coverage`) files appear in `TestResults/`.

---

## Phase 4 — Azure DevOps pipeline changes

### 4.1 Replace `VSTest@3` with `DotNetCoreCLI@2`

```yaml
# BEFORE
- task: VSTest@3
  inputs:
    testAssemblyVer2: '**\*Tests.dll'
    runSettingsFile: 'test.runsettings'
    codeCoverageEnabled: true

# AFTER
- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
  displayName: 'Run tests'
  inputs:
    command: 'test'
    projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'
    arguments: >-
      --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults
      --no-build
      --configuration Release
```

### 4.2 Publish TRX results

```yaml
- task: PublishTestResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish test results'
  condition: always()
  inputs:
    testResultsFormat: 'VSTest'   # TRX format
    testResultsFiles: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/**/*.trx'
    mergeTestResults: true
```

### 4.3 Publish code coverage

```yaml
- task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish code coverage'
  condition: always()
  inputs:
    summaryFileLocation: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/**/coverage.cobertura.xml'
```

---

## Phase 5 — Cleanup and hardening

- Remove any remaining `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` references (MSTest 4.x doesn't need it).
- Remove `MSTest.TestAdapter` and `MSTest.TestFramework` individual references.
- Delete `.runsettings` if no longer needed.
- Pin package versions in `Directory.Packages.props` if using Central Package Management.
- Update CI caches if they key on package hashes.

---

## Risks / Notes

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Tests that rely on VSTest-specific APIs (`TestContext.DeploymentDirectory`, deployment items) | Audit usage; MTP doesn't support deployment items — copy files via build targets instead. |
| Custom VSTest loggers | Replace with MTP extensions or the TRX extension above. |
| `.runsettings` with complex coverage filters | Migrate to `microsoft.codecoverage.json` config file. |
| Parallel test execution differences | MTP runs each project exe in parallel by default; within-project parallelism needs `<TestingParallelism>`. |
| Third-party analyzers that hook into VSTest | Verify compatibility; most major tools support MTP now. |

---

2. Remove old VSTest packages (`MSTest.TestAdapter`, `MSTest.TestFramework`, `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`)
3. Add TRX + CodeCoverage extension packages
4. Remove/migrate `.runsettings`
5. Verify locally — build, test, TRX output, coverage output
6. Update Azure DevOps pipeline: replace `VSTest@3` → `DotNetCoreCLI@2` + `PublishTestResults@2` + `PublishCodeCoverageResults@2`
7. Clean up obsolete files and references
